purpose: Ninth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering a completed neighbor-target command buffer delivered onto an output channel.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular neighbor-delivery command-buffer node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular neighbor-delivery command-buffer node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular neighbor-delivery command-buffer node.

layers.summary: A matching one-hot standard input completes buffer 10101, decodes it as neighbor-b/proc-l-init, and delivers the command token to output channel 1.
layers.boundary: This layer states only neighbor command-buffer delivery; it does not execute the delivered token on a recipient cell.

layers.summary: The neighbor-delivery command-buffer node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete recipient-side command execution circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one neighbor-target command-buffer delivery.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0044/ADR-0045 delivery claim and adds no neighbor consumption behavior.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested delivery behavior.
